text.title: PHP Eloquent ORM Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Eloquent codebases.

antipattern.symptom: List endpoint with 50 orders fires 51 queries (1 + 50); p99 latency 3s.
antipattern.root-cause: `$orders = Order::all(); foreach ($orders as $o) { echo $o->customer->name; }` — customer accessed lazily inside loop.
antipattern.fix: `$orders = Order::with('customer')->get();` — one extra query for all customers.

antipattern.symptom: User registration accepts arbitrary keys; `is_admin=1` in request body creates admin user.
antipattern.fix: Declare `$fillable = ['name', 'email', 'password']` on User; `is_admin` cannot be set via mass-assign.

antipattern.symptom: `$user->active === true` returns false; admin panel checkbox bug; `$user->active === '1'` works.
antipattern.root-cause: active column is tinyint(1); Eloquent returns string by default.

antipattern.symptom: Order create followed by inventory decrement; mid-call DB hiccup leaves order without inventory deduction; manual reconciliation.
antipattern.fix: `DB::transaction(function () { $order = Order::create(...); InventoryItem::where('sku', $sku)->decrement('qty'); });` — both rolled back on failure.
